### Top 10 Call Topics

The ten most common call topics in the call centre topic data.

This is useful for quickly identifying the most common reasons people contact the
call centre.

*Alternative names*:

- top 10 call reasons
- most common call topics
- top contact drivers
- main reasons for calling
- top caller issues

**Calculation.** Count records by cleaned topic, calculate topic share, and
return the top 10 by topic count.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## SQL

```sql
WITH topic_counts AS (
    SELECT
        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(statobject), '\s+', ' ') AS call_topic,
        COUNT(*) AS topic_count
    FROM call_centre.weekly_topic_dump
    GROUP BY REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(statobject), '\s+', ' ')
),

topic_shares AS (
    SELECT
        call_topic,
        topic_count,
        CAST(topic_count AS DOUBLE)
            / NULLIF(SUM(topic_count) OVER (), 0) AS topic_share
    FROM topic_counts
)

SELECT
    call_topic,
    topic_count,
    topic_share
FROM topic_shares
ORDER BY topic_count DESC
LIMIT 10;
```

## R

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("call_centre", "weekly_topic_dump")) |>
  mutate(
    call_topic = str_squish(statobject)
  ) |>
  count(call_topic, name = "topic_count") |>
  mutate(
    topic_share = as.double(topic_count) / sum(topic_count)
  ) |>
  slice_max(
    topic_count,
    n = 10,
    with_ties = FALSE
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(topic_count))
```

:::

*Interpretation notes.* The top 10 view is useful for quick reporting but may hide
important lower-volume issues. In cases where there are not many calls, it may be
useful to limit this list to a top 5, or to truncate ties at the end of the list.
